title: 10 best herbal teas for frizzy hair
intro_desc: Herbal teas for frizzy hair are infusions made from various herbs that can help improve the condition of frizzy hair. These teas are rich in nutrients, antioxidants, and anti-inflammatory properties that can nourish the scalp, strengthen hair follicles, and promote healthy hair growth. Examples of herbal teas that can help with frizzy hair include chamomile, rosemary, nettle, and horsetail. Chamomile tea can help soothe a dry, itchy scalp, while rosemary tea can stimulate blood flow to the scalp, promoting hair growth. Nettle tea is rich in vitamins and minerals that can strengthen hair follicles, reducing breakage and frizz. Horsetail tea is high in silica, which can help improve the texture and shine of hair. By using herbal teas for frizzy hair, individuals can achieve smoother, more manageable hair, leading to improved self-confidence and overall quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tea helps with frizzy hair because it contains anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties that can improve the health of your hair and scalp. The tea is rich in vitamins and minerals, including magnesium, potassium, and calcium, which can help strengthen hair follicles, reduce hair breakage, and promote hair growth. Moreover, yarrow tea has been shown to balance the pH level of the scalp, which can help reduce frizz and add shine to your hair. Drinking yarrow tea or using it as a rinse after washing your hair can help smooth the hair cuticle, reduce frizz, and leave your hair looking soft, silky, and more manageable.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tea's anti-inflammatory properties, due to the presence of flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones, can help soothe a dry, itchy scalp, which can contribute to frizzy hair.
Astringent: The astringent properties of yarrow tea, from tannins and volatile oils, can help reduce excess oil production on the scalp, preventing frizz and promoting healthier hair.
Antioxidant: Yarrow tea's high antioxidant content, thanks to phenolic acids and flavonoids, can help protect the hair from environmental damage, promoting hair health and reducing frizz.

Circulation-boosting: Yarrow tea promotes blood circulation in the scalp, due to its lactones and essential oils, which can help stimulate hair growth and reduce frizz by strengthening hair follicles.
Moisturizing: Yarrow tea's moisturizing properties, from its polysaccharides and flavonoids, can help hydrate and nourish the hair, reducing frizz and leaving hair smooth and manageable.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water and add 2 tablespoons of dried yarrow flowers, simmering for 10 minutes.
Turn off the heat, cover the pot, and let the mixture steep for an additional 20 minutes.
Strain the liquid to remove the yarrow flowers, reserving the tea.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
After shampooing and conditioning, pour the yarrow tea over your hair, massaging it into your scalp, then let it sit for 10 minutes before rinsing.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in vitamins and minerals, yarrow flowers help nourish the scalp and reduce frizz.
Leaves: Containing anti-inflammatory properties, yarrow leaves soothe the scalp and promote healthy hair growth.
Seeds: High in essential fatty acids, yarrow seeds moisturize and strengthen hair, reducing frizz and promoting shine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet flag tea helps with frizzy hair because it contains valuable nutrients that promote hair growth and reduce frizz. This aromatic plant, also known as vetiver, is rich in antioxidants, proteins, and minerals that nourish the scalp and strengthen hair follicles. Regular consumption of sweet flag tea can help improve blood circulation in the scalp, which in turn encourages the production of natural oils that moisturize and smooth frizzy hair. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of sweet flag tea can help soothe an itchy scalp, reducing dandruff and further contributing to smoother, more manageable hair.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Sweet flag tea's anti-inflammatory properties, due to the presence of compounds like asarone and eugenol, can help reduce scalp inflammation, promoting healthier hair growth and reducing frizz.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of sweet flag tea, from constituents such as polyphenols and flavonoids, protect hair follicles from oxidative stress, resulting in less frizz and healthier hair.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of sweet flag tea, attributed to compounds like asarone and eugenol, can help maintain a healthy scalp by inhibiting the growth of harmful bacteria and fungi, which can contribute to frizzy hair.

Circulation-enhancing: Sweet flag tea's ability to improve blood circulation in the scalp, due to its essential oils, allows for better nutrient delivery to hair follicles, promoting hair growth and reducing frizz.
Moisturizing: The moisturizing properties of sweet flag tea, resulting from its mucilage content, help hydrate and nourish the hair, reducing frizz and leaving hair smooth and manageable.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water and add 2 tablespoons of dried sweet flag (Acorus calamus) root, then let it simmer for 15 minutes.
Turn off the heat, cover the pot and let the mixture steep for an additional 30 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the solid particles, then let it cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Shampoo your hair as usual, then pour the cooled sweet flag tea over your hair, massaging it into your scalp.
Leave the tea in your hair for 10-15 minutes, then rinse it out thoroughly with warm water.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the sweet flag plant contain fragrant essential oils and phenolic compounds that can help to strengthen and smooth frizzy hair.
Leaves: The leaves of the sweet flag plant are rich in nutrients and antioxidants that can help to nourish and protect the hair, reducing frizz and promoting shine.
Flowers: The flowers of the sweet flag plant contain flavonoids and other compounds that can help to improve hair elasticity and reduce frizz, leaving hair soft and manageable.
Seeds: The seeds of the sweet flag plant are a good source of essential fatty acids, which can help to moisturize and condition the hair, reducing frizz and promoting healthy hair growth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tea helps with frizzy hair because it contains mucilage, a substance that can moisturize and soften hair, reducing frizz and increasing shine. The tea is made from the marshmallow plant, which is rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to soothe and protect the scalp. This can lead to healthier hair growth and reduced frizz. Additionally, the tea can help to detangle hair, making it easier to manage and style. Drinking marshmallow tea or using it as a rinse can provide these benefits, leaving you with smoother, more manageable hair.
remedy_properties: Demulcent property: Marshmallow tea contains mucilage, a substance that creates a protective film on the hair shaft, helping to smooth frizzy hair.
Anti-inflammatory property: The flavonoids and phenolic acids in marshmallow tea can reduce scalp inflammation, promoting healthier hair growth and reducing frizz.
Emollient property: The high mucilage content in marshmallow tea can soften and moisturize the hair, reducing frizz caused by dryness and brittleness.
Hair conditioning property: Marshmallow tea can enhance hair's ability to retain moisture, making it more manageable and less prone to frizz.
Antioxidant property: The antioxidants in marshmallow tea, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can protect the hair from environmental stressors, maintaining hair health and reducing frizz.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of water, 1 tablespoon of dried nettle leaves, and 1 tablespoon of dried horsetail herb; bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 15 minutes.
Add 2 tablespoons of dried chamomile flowers to the pot and let the mixture steep for an additional 5 minutes.
Strain the herbal mixture into a bowl, then add 2 cups of strong chamomile tea and 1/4 cup of pure honey; stir until the honey is fully dissolved.
Pour the tea into a heat-safe container and let it cool to room temperature before refrigerating; allow the tea to chill for at least 2 hours, or until cold.
To use, mix 1/2 cup of the chilled tea with 1/2 cup of warm water and pour the mixture over 1 cup of marshmallow root; let the mixture soak for 30 minutes before rinsing your hair with the tea.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in nutrients, marshmallow leaves help to moisturize and detangle frizzy hair.
Flowers: Marshmallow flowers contain mucilage, which coats the hair shaft and adds shine.
Roots: Marshmallow roots are high in starch and create a soothing, smoothing effect on frizzy hair.
Seeds: Marshmallow seeds contain oils that help to nourish and condition dry, frizzy hair.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock tea helps with frizzy hair because it is rich in fatty acids, vitamins, and minerals that nourish and strengthen the hair follicles, promoting healthy hair growth. The tea's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties also help to soothe the scalp, reduce dandruff, and improve blood circulation, which can help to reduce frizz and promote shiny, smooth hair. Additionally, burdock tea contains high levels of silica, a mineral that can help to strengthen hair and reduce breakage, contributing to overall hair health and reducing frizz.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Burdock tea's anti-inflammatory properties, due to the presence of tannins and essential oils, can help soothe and reduce scalp inflammation, which can contribute to frizzy hair.
Moisturizing: Burdock tea contains mucilage, a type of polysaccharide, which can help moisturize and smooth the hair cuticle, reducing frizz.
Scalp stimulation: Burdock tea's active constituents, such as volatile oils and fatty acids, can help improve blood circulation in the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and minimizing frizz.
Detoxification: Burdock tea has detoxifying properties, as it helps eliminate toxins and impurities from the body, including the scalp, which can help prevent and reduce frizzy hair.
Antioxidant: Burdock tea contains antioxidants, such as phenolic acids and flavonoids, which can help protect the hair from oxidative stress and damage, maintaining its smoothness and reducing frizz.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 2 cups of water and 2 tablespoons of dried burdock root.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Remove the pot from heat and strain the liquid to remove the solid burdock root.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Pour the burdock tea over your hair, massaging it into your scalp and throughout your hair. Leave it on for 15-20 minutes before rinsing it out.
remedy_parts: Roots: Burdock roots are rich in fatty oils and phytosterols that can help nourish and smooth frizzy hair.
Seeds: Burdock seeds contain essential fatty acids and antioxidants that can help moisturize and strengthen frizzy hair.
Leaves: Burdock leaves are a good source of vitamins and minerals that can help promote healthy hair growth and reduce frizz.
Flowers: Burdock flowers contain flavonoids and tannins that can help soothe the scalp and reduce inflammation, promoting smooth and shiny hair.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood tea helps with frizzy hair because it is rich in nutrients and antioxidants that can improve the health of your hair and scalp. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce scalp inflammation, which can be a cause of frizzy hair. Wormwood tea also contains a compound called artemisinin, which can help stimulate hair growth and reduce hair fall, resulting in smoother and more manageable hair. Additionally, wormwood tea can help balance the pH level of your hair and scalp, which can help reduce frizz and add shine to your hair. Drinking wormwood tea or using it as a rinse can provide these benefits and help you achieve smoother and more beautiful hair.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Wormwood tea's anti-inflammatory properties, due to compounds like alpha-thujone and beta-thujone, can help reduce scalp inflammation, promoting healthier hair growth and reducing frizz caused by irritation.

Antimicrobial: Wormwood tea's antimicrobial properties, attributed to compounds like camphor and camphene, can help maintain a clean and healthy scalp by preventing the growth of microorganisms that may contribute to frizzy hair.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of wormwood tea, from flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help protect the hair and scalp from oxidative stress, reducing damage and frizz.

Hair growth promotion: Wormwood tea stimulates hair growth, thanks to compounds like sesquiterpene lactones, which can help improve circulation and nourish hair follicles, leading to stronger, smoother hair.
Scalp circulation: Wormwood tea improves scalp circulation due to its stimulating properties, allowing better nutrient absorption and promoting healthier, frizz-free hair growth.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 2 cups of water and 2 tablespoons of dried wormwood herb, then bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce heat, cover the pot, and let it simmer for 15-20 minutes to allow the herb to release its properties.
After simmering, strain the tea into a bowl or container, discarding the used wormwood herb.
Allow the wormwood tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Before bedtime, thoroughly wet your frizzy hair with the cooled wormwood tea, then massage it into your scalp and leave it on overnight. Rinse your hair in the morning.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in nutrients, the leaves help to nourish and smooth frizzy hair.
Flowers: Contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that promote hair health and reduce frizz.
Seeds: High in essential oils, seeds help to moisturize and strengthen hair, reducing frizz and promoting shine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Silver birch tea helps with frizzy hair because it is rich in vitamins and minerals that promote hair health. It contains vitamin C, which is essential for collagen production, a protein that strengthens hair and reduces frizz. The tea also has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe a dry, itchy scalp, which can contribute to frizz. Additionally, silver birch tea is a natural source of silica, a mineral that can help to strengthen hair and reduce breakage, resulting in smoother, more manageable hair. Drinking silver birch tea or using it as a rinse after washing your hair can help to tame frizz and promote healthy, shiny hair.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Silver birch tea helps reduce scalp inflammation, which can cause frizzy hair by triggering excess oil production and damaging hair follicles. This property is due to the presence of betulinic acid.
Hair strengthening: The tea contains essential minerals like potassium, calcium, and magnesium that strengthen hair fibers, reducing frizz caused by weak and brittle strands.

Detoxification: Silver birch tea promotes scalp detoxification by stimulating blood circulation and eliminating toxins, which can lead to healthier, less frizzy hair. The detoxifying effect is attributed to the flavonoids and tannins in the tea.

Moisturizing: The tea has natural moisturizing properties that help hydrate and nourish the scalp and hair, reducing frizz caused by dryness. This is due to the presence of amino acids and organic acids.

Dandruff reduction: Silver birch tea inhibits the growth of Malassezia furfur, a fungus responsible for dandruff, which can contribute to frizzy hair. This property is attributed to the betulinic acid and other triterpenes found in the tea.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 liter of water and 3 tablespoons of silver birch leaves, then bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 15 minutes.
Add 2 tablespoons of dried nettle leaves and 1 tablespoon of dried rosemary leaves, then let it steep for an additional 10 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool down before using it as a rinse after shampooing your hair.
Store the remaining tea in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in nutrients and antioxidants, silver birch leaves help to nourish and strengthen hair, reducing frizz.
Bark: Containing essential oils and tannins, silver birch bark can help to soothe the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing frizz.
Buds: Packed with vitamins and minerals, silver birch buds can help to stimulate hair growth, improve hair texture, and reduce frizz.
Seeds: High in fatty acids and proteins, silver birch seeds can help to moisturize and condition hair, reducing frizz and promoting shine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tea helps with frizzy hair because it is rich in vitamin E and antioxidants that promote hair growth and reduce hair damage. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties help soothe and calm the scalp, reducing frizz and promoting healthy hair growth. The tea also contains flavonoids, which help strengthen hair follicles, preventing breakage and split ends. Regular consumption of pot marigold tea can lead to smoother, shinier, and more manageable hair, as it helps lock in moisture, leaving hair looking and feeling healthier.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tea helps reduce scalp inflammation, which can cause frizzy hair by causing irritation and dryness. This property is due to the presence of flavonoids and carotenoids.
Moisturizing: Pot marigold tea has moisturizing properties that help hydrate the hair and reduce frizz. This is due to the high content of mucilage in the plant.
Antioxidant: Pot marigold tea contains antioxidants that help protect the hair from damage caused by free radicals, which can lead to frizzy hair. The antioxidants present include vitamin C and E.

Anti-fungal: Pot marigold tea has anti-fungal properties that help keep the scalp healthy and free from fungal infections, which can cause frizzy hair. This property is due to the presence of flavonoids and essential oils.

Soothing: Pot marigold tea has a soothing effect on the scalp, which can help reduce itchiness and dandruff, both of which can contribute to frizzy hair. This is due to the presence of anti-inflammatory compounds and essential oils.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 2 cups of water and 1 tablespoon of dried marigold flowers, then bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce heat to low, cover the pot, and let it simmer for 15 minutes to allow the marigold flowers to release their nutrients.
After simmering, remove the pot from heat and let it cool for about 10 minutes, or until the tea is warm but not hot.
Strain the tea into a glass or mug, discarding the marigold flowers.
Apply the marigold tea to your frizzy hair, massaging it into your scalp and working it through your hair strands, then let it sit for 30 minutes before rinsing it out.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Pot marigold flowers are rich in flavonoids and carotenoids, which can help to condition the hair and reduce frizz.
Leaves: The leaves of the pot marigold plant contain high levels of vitamin C and antioxidants, which can help to strengthen the hair and reduce frizz.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds are a good source of essential fatty acids, which can help to moisturize the hair and reduce frizz.
Stems: The stems of the pot marigold plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds, which can help to soothe the scalp and reduce frizz.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea helps with frizzy hair because it is rich in antioxidants, which can help to protect the hair from damage caused by free radicals. These antioxidants can help to strengthen the hair and reduce breakage, which can help to reduce the appearance of frizz. Additionally, white tea contains anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe a dry, itchy scalp, which can also contribute to frizz. The tea can be used as a rinse or added to conditioners and other hair treatments to help smooth and detangle the hair, leaving it looking shiny and smooth.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: White tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce scalp inflammation, which can contribute to frizzy hair. This property is important because it promotes a healthy scalp environment, allowing for smoother and more manageable hair. The active constituents responsible for this property include tannins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids.

Antioxidant: White tea contains high levels of antioxidants, which help protect the hair from environmental damage and oxidative stress, reducing frizz. Antioxidants are crucial for frizzy hair because they prevent free radical damage, preserving the hair's natural moisture and elasticity. The primary antioxidants in white tea are epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG), epicatechin gallate (ECG), and epicatechin (EC).

Anti-androgenic: White tea has anti-androgenic properties, which can help regulate hormonal imbalances that may lead to frizzy hair. This property is important because it promotes a balanced hormonal state, reducing hair frizz caused by hormonal fluctuations. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and polyphenols.

Moisturizing: White tea has moisturizing properties that help hydrate the hair and scalp, reducing frizz. Moisturizing is essential for frizzy hair because it keeps the hair strands flexible and less prone to breakage and split ends. The active constituents responsible for this property include amino acids, vitamins, and minerals present in white tea.

Hair growth promotion: White tea promotes hair growth, which can help reduce frizz by ensuring that the hair is healthy and well-nourished. This property is important because it helps maintain an optimal hair growth cycle, preventing hair breakage and thinning, which can contribute to frizz. The active constituents responsible for this property include catechins, epigallocatechin-3-gallate (EGCG), and theanine.
remedy_recipe: Boil 2 cups of water and add 2 teaspoons of dried organic white tea leaves; let it steep for 15 minutes to extract the tea's nutrients.
In a separate pot, bring 1 cup of water to a boil, then add 1/4 cup of dried chamomile flowers, letting it simmer for 10 minutes to infuse the water with calming chamomile.
Strain both tea mixtures and combine them in a heat-resistant bowl; allow it to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Stir in 1 tablespoon of raw organic honey until fully dissolved, providing moisture and shine to your hair.

Thoroughly wet your hair, then slowly pour the tea and honey mixture over your hair, massaging it into your scalp and working it through your strands; let it sit for 20 minutes before rinsing with warm water.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and nutrients, white tea leaves help to strengthen hair follicles and reduce frizz.
Buds: High in flavonoids, white tea buds promote hair growth and help to smooth frizzy hair.
Flowers: Infused with vitamins and minerals, white tea flowers aid in hair repair and add shine to frizzy hair.
Seeds: Packed with essential fatty acids, white tea seeds help to moisturize and tame frizzy hair.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Caraway tea helps with frizzy hair because it is rich in vitamins and minerals that promote hair growth and reduce hair breakage. The antioxidants present in caraway tea help to strengthen hair follicles, while its moisturizing properties help to smooth frizz and add shine to hair. Additionally, caraway tea contains anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe a dry, itchy scalp, which can contribute to frizzy hair. Regularly consuming caraway tea or using it as a rinse can help to improve the overall health and appearance of your hair, leaving it looking smooth, shiny, and more manageable.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Caraway tea's anti-inflammatory properties, due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help soothe scalp irritations and reduce frizz caused by inflammation.
Antioxidant: The rich antioxidant content, primarily from carvone and limonene, helps protect the hair from environmental damage and oxidative stress, reducing frizz and promoting healthier hair.
Moisturizing: Caraway tea's moisturizing properties, attributed to its essential oils and mucilage content, help hydrate and smooth the hair cuticle, reducing frizz and increasing shine.

Scalp stimulation: Caraway tea promotes blood circulation in the scalp, thanks to the presence of carvacrol and thymol, which encourages healthy hair growth and reduces frizz caused by poor scalp health.
Dandruff reduction: Caraway tea's antifungal properties, owing to the presence of monoterpenes and sesquiterpenes, help control dandruff and maintain a balanced scalp, minimizing frizz and flakiness.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water and add 2 tablespoons of dried caraway seeds, letting it simmer for 15 minutes.
Turn off the heat and let the mixture steep for an additional 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the caraway seeds, reserving the liquid.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
After shampooing, pour the caraway tea over your hair, massaging it into your scalp, and let it sit for 10-15 minutes before rinsing it out.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Caraway seeds are rich in essential oils and antioxidants, making them an excellent ingredient for nourishing and taming frizzy hair.
Leaves: High in vitamins and minerals, caraway leaves promote hair growth and help reduce frizz by moisturizing the hair shaft.
Flowers: Caraway flowers contain anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing frizz.
Rhizomes: Caraway rhizomes are a great source of nutrients that strengthen hair follicles, reducing breakage and frizz.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Gotu kola tea helps with frizzy hair because it is rich in antioxidants and nutrients that promote healthy hair growth. It contains vitamin C and E, which are essential for collagen production, a protein that strengthens hair follicles and reduces hair breakage. Additionally, gotu kola tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe a dry, itchy scalp, which can contribute to frizzy hair. The tea also contains triterpenoids, which have been shown to improve circulation and promote blood flow to the scalp, delivering essential nutrients to hair follicles. This increased blood flow can help reduce hair fall and promote hair growth, leaving you with smoother, more manageable hair. Regular consumption of gotu kola tea can help keep your hair hydrated, strong, and frizz-free.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Gotu kola tea can help reduce inflammation in the scalp, promoting a healthy environment for hair growth and reducing frizz caused by inflammation. This property is due to the presence of triterpenoid saponins, such as asiaticoside and madecassoside.

Promotes collagen synthesis: Gotu kola tea stimulates collagen production, which can strengthen hair follicles and reduce breakage, leading to smoother and less frizzy hair. The active constituents responsible for this property are triterpenoid saponins and flavonoids.

Enhances circulation: Gotu kola tea improves blood flow to the scalp, ensuring that hair follicles receive essential nutrients for healthy hair growth and reducing frizz caused by poor circulation. The main active constituents responsible for this property are triterpenoid saponins and essential oils.

Antioxidant properties: Gotu kola tea contains antioxidants that protect the hair and scalp from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthier hair and reducing frizz. The primary active constituents responsible for this property are flavonoids, tannins, and essential oils.

Moisturizing properties: Gotu kola tea helps lock in moisture, keeping the hair hydrated and reducing frizz caused by dryness. This property is attributed to the presence of polysaccharides and essential oils in gotu kola.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water for 5 minutes.
Add 1 tablespoon of dried gotu kola leaves and let it steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the leaves, then let it cool.
After shampooing, pour the cooled tea over your hair and massage it into your scalp.
Leave it on for 15 minutes, then rinse it out with water.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and nutrients, gotu kola leaves help promote hair growth and reduce frizz by nourishing the scalp and strengthening hair follicles.
Stems: Containing triterpenoid saponins, gotu kola stems can help improve blood circulation in the scalp, reducing frizz and promoting healthy hair growth.
Rhizomes: Packed with essential fatty acids and flavonoids, gotu kola rhizomes can moisturize and protect hair, reducing frizz and adding shine.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat frizzy hair with herbal teas is by rinsing your hair with cooled, brewed tea. Chamomile tea adds shine and silkiness to dry hair, while green tea seals in moisture and adds luster. For a strengthening treatment, use rosemary tea, which is also known to promote hair growth. After shampooing, pour the tea over your hair, massaging it into your scalp. Let it sit for a few minutes before rinsing with cold water to seal the cuticle and add shine. Regular use of herbal tea rinses can help combat frizz, leaving you with smooth, manageable hair.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to frizzy hair that are treated with herbal teas include dandruff and dry, itchy scalp. Certain herbal teas, like chamomile and nettle, have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe an itchy scalp and reduce dandruff. Green tea, rich in antioxidants, can promote hair growth and reduce hair loss. Peppermint tea can help to stimulate the scalp and improve blood circulation, which can lead to stronger, healthier hair. These herbal teas can be used as a rinse after shampooing or steeped and applied directly to the scalp. However, it's always best to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new treatment.
